_description: Configure C++ pybind11 modules and calculator definitions for high-performance native computation

cpp_manager._enabled_note: Set to true to enable automatic C++ module loading and calculator initialization
cpp_manager._auto_load_note: If true, C++ modules are loaded when application.properties is loaded
cpp_manager._config_check_note: Check for config file changes every 600 seconds (10 minutes)

modules.name: dishtayantra_cpp
modules._path_note: Path to the .so/.pyd file. If null, expects module in Python path

build._build_note: Build settings for automatic compilation (requires CMake and pybind11)

lmdb._lmdb_note: LMDB zero-copy transport for large payloads (>10KB)

calculators.name: PassthroughCalculator
calculators.description: Passes input data through unchanged - useful for testing and benchmarking

calculators.name: MathCalculator
calculators.description: High-performance mathematical operations (sum, product, mean, variance)

calculators.name: StatisticsCalculator
calculators.description: Statistical analysis (mean, std, min, max, percentiles)

calculators.name: DataValidationCalculator
calculators.description: Fast data validation with regex and type checking

calculators.name: TradePricingCalculator
calculators.description: Trade pricing with commission and fee calculations

calculators.name: RiskMetricsCalculator
calculators.description: Financial risk metrics (VaR, Sharpe, Beta, Greeks)

performance._simd_note: Enable SIMD optimizations where available (AVX, SSE)
performance._thread_pool_note: Number of threads for parallel C++ operations

_documentation.overview: This configuration defines C++ pybind11 modules and calculators available to DishtaYantra DAGs
_documentation.module_structure: Each module is a compiled .so/.pyd file containing one or more calculator classes
_documentation.calculator_interface: C++ calculators must implement: constructor(name, config), calculate(data), details()
